recalls.Description: LShome is recalling its 3-Pack Smoke Detector Fire Alarms due to a critical failure in the warning system during a fire, which could lead to serious injury or death. These battery-operated alarms feature a light sound warning and a test button, and are designed in a circular white shape. The model number 'XG-7D04-KZ9Z' and SKU 'CX-50YP-A5VN' can be found on the bottom of the device.
recalls.Title: LShome 3-Pack Smoke Detector Fire Alarms Recalled Due to Inadequate Fire Warning System

Images.Caption: Recalled LSHOME 3-Pack Smoke Detector Fire Alarm packaging
Recalled LSHOME 3-Pack Smoke Detector Fire Alarms

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from February 2024 to December 2025 for approximately $30.

Hazards.Name: Delayed alarm response due to high sensing threshold, increasing fire risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the alarms immediately and contact lmm15957491237@163.com for a full refund via Amazon.com. Dispose of the smoke alarms in household trash.

recalls.metaDescription: LShome recalls smoke detector alarms due to failure to warn in fires, posing serious risks. Contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Apollo America has issued a recall for its Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detectors, model number 51000-600. These detectors, available in white, may fail to alert users to the presence of smoke or carbon monoxide, which can lead to serious health risks, including smoke inhalation and carbon monoxide poisoning. The front of the unit displays a 'Replace by' date between June and October 2034, while the back features the 'Apollo' branding along with the model number.
recalls.Title: Apollo America Recalls Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detectors Due to Safety Alert Failures; Risk of Serious Injury or Death
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Vivint at 800-281-9667, email recalls@vivint.com, or visit https://www.vivint.com/smoke-co-recall for additional details.

Products.Name: Apollo America Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector Model 51000-600

Images.Caption: Recalled Apollo America Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector
Back of recalled Apollo America Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector
Recalled Apollo America Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector

Manufacturers.Name: Apollo America Inc., of Auburn Hills, Michigan

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.Vivint.com and through Vivint's direct sales from June to October 2024, priced between $50 and $100.

Hazards.Name: Potential malfunction leading to failure in alerting users about fire or carbon monoxide leaks, risking serious health issues.

Remedies.Name: Vivint is providing a free replacement detector to all affected customers. Users should continue using the recalled units until the replacement arrives and install it immediately upon receipt. For disposal, consumers should mark the original detector as 'recalled', remove its batteries, and follow local regulations for battery disposal.

recalls.metaDescription: Apollo America recalls smoke and carbon monoxide detectors due to alert failures, posing serious risks. Free replacements offered. Contact Vivint for details.

recalls.Description: Three61 is recalling its Samurai-branded mini smoke alarms, model SM1, due to a critical malfunction that prevents them from alerting users to fire hazards. These compact smoke detectors, measuring approximately 2.5 inches on each side, were sold in sets of two or three and included lithium-ion batteries, adhesive stickers, screws, mounting plates, and an instruction manual. The alarms feature the word 'Smoke' on the front and 'installed on' on the back, with the model number found on the packaging.
recalls.Title: Three61 Recalls Samurai Mini Smoke Alarms Due to Fire Alert Failure; Sold Exclusively by HSN
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Three61 via email at recall@samuraibrands.com or visit www.samuraibrands.com/recall. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.samuraibrands.com and click on the recall banner at the top of the page for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm - front
Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm - back
Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm – packaging
Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm - packaging

Injuries.Name: Three61 has received eight reports of the alarms failing to activate in the presence of smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through HSN nationwide and online at HSN.com from July 2020 to November 2024, priced between $40 and $50 based on the quantity purchased.

Importers.Name: Three61 LLC, of Palm Harbor, Florida

Hazards.Name: Malfunctioning smoke alarms that fail to alert users to fire, increasing the risk of smoke inhalation or fatality.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Three61 for a free replacement alarm, including shipping. To initiate the process, they must mark the recalled smoke alarm with 'Recalled' using a permanent marker and submit a photo of it on the recall website. Once registered, a replacement alarm will be promptly shipped. Consumers should keep the recalled alarms installed until the replacement arrives and should install the new alarms immediately upon receipt. Three61 is also reaching out directly to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Three61 recalls Samurai Mini Smoke Alarms due to fire alert failure. Contact for a free replacement alarm. Stay safe with proper smoke detection.

recalls.Description: Academy Sports + Outdoors has announced a recall of the Redfield 12-Gun and 18-Gun Fireproof Safes due to a significant safety concern. The 12-Gun Safe is identified by model number 179498, while the 18-Gun Safe carries model number 179499. Both safes feature 'Redfield' printed on the front door above the keypad, with the model name and serial number located on a red label at the bottom right corner of the door. The 12-Gun Safe measures 53 inches in height, 15 inches in width, and 16 inches in depth, while the 18-Gun Safe is 55 inches high, 18 inches wide, and 18 inches deep. Affected serial numbers include various combinations starting with G02B, G03B for the 12-Gun Safe and G10A, G11A, G12A, G01B, G02B, G03B for the 18-Gun Safe.
recalls.Title: Academy Sports + Outdoors Issues Recall for Redfield 12-Gun and 18-Gun Fireproof Safes Due to Serious Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Academy Sports + Outdoors at 888-922-2336 from 7 a.m. to 10 p.m. CT, email customerservice@academy.com, or visit www.academy.com/product-recalls. Additional details can be found by clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Academy Sports + Outdoors Redfield 12-Gun Fireproof Safe
Recalled Academy Sports + Outdoors Redfield 18-Gun Fireproof Safe
Recalled Academy Sports + Outdoors Redfield Gun Fireproof Safe serial number location

Retailers.Name: Available at Academy Sports + Outdoors locations and online at academy.com from February 2024 to August 2024, priced between $350 and $600.

Importers.Name: Academy Ltd., d/b/a Academy Sports + Outdoors, of Katy, Texas

Hazards.Name: Potential failure of internal locking plates allowing unauthorized access, leading to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the safes immediately and return them to any Academy Sports + Outdoors store for a complimentary replacement or refund. Alternatively, customers can contact Academy's Customer Care for assistance.

recalls.metaDescription: Academy Sports + Outdoors recalls Redfield Fireproof Safes due to serious safety risks. Stop using and return for a refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: New Cosmos USA is recalling the DeNova Detect combination natural gas and carbon monoxide alarms, model DD622NCV. These alarms are labeled with 'DeNova Detect, Natural Gas + Carbon Monoxide' on the front, and the model number can be found on the top side, while the serial number is located on the right side of the device. The alarms may fail to alert users to dangerous levels of natural gas or carbon monoxide due to a malfunction that causes them to enter sleep mode after a brief alert period.
recalls.Title: New Cosmos USA Recalls DeNova Detect Natural Gas and Carbon Monoxide Alarms for Safety Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact New Cosmos USA Inc. at 888-447-6016 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email safety@DeNovaDetect.com. Additional details are available online at www.denovadetect.com under the 'Recalls' section or at www.denovadetect.com/safetynotice-dd622.

Products.Name: DeNova Detect Combination Natural Gas and Carbon Monoxide Alarm

Images.Caption: Recalled Natural Gas + CO Alarm
Location of Model Number of Alarm
Location of Serial Number of Alarm

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's stores nationwide and online through Amazon.com, Lowes.com, and Denovadetect.com from December 2023 to July 2024, priced between $90 and $102.

Distributors.Name: New Cosmos USA Inc., of Lisle, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide poisoning due to failure to alert after initial warning

Remedies.Name: Register online for a prepaid shipping package and replacement alarm

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of DeNova Detect natural gas and carbon monoxide alarms due to safety risks. Contact New Cosmos USA for replacement information.

recalls.Description: The recall affects the Sanctuary Quick Access Biometric Home Safe and the Sports Afield Home Defense 4-Gun Biometric Safe, which are designed for firearm and valuables storage. The Sanctuary safe measures 10 inches in height, 14 inches in width, and 10 inches in depth, capable of holding approximately two firearms. In contrast, the Sports Afield safe, measuring 55 inches high, 12 inches wide, and 11 inches deep, can accommodate up to four firearms and features the Sports Afield logo on its front.
recalls.Title: Recall of Sanctuary Quick Access and Sports Afield Biometric Gun Safes Due to Serious Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach SA Consumer Products at 855-215-5822 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, or via email at saconsumer6983@sedgwick.com. More information is available online at sabiometricsaferecall.expertinquiry.com or by visiting www.saproducts.com and selecting the 'Recall' tab on the homepage.

Products.Name: Sanctuary Quick Access Biometric Home Safe

Images.Caption: Recalled Sanctuary Quick Access Biometric Home Safe
Recalled Sports Afield Home Defense 4-Gun Biometric Safe

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of 77 reports of the safe being opened by unauthorized users. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Costco and various retailers nationwide, as well as online at Costco.com, priced between $75 and $100 for the Sanctuary safe. The Sports Afield safe was sold at sporting goods stores and online for $500 to $600. Sales occurred from July 2020 through November 2023.

Importers.Name: SA Consumer Products Inc., of Rolling Meadows, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Biometric lock vulnerability allowing unauthorized access, leading to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the biometric feature immediately, remove the batteries, and use the key for firearm storage. Contact SA Consumer Products for a free repair kit.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Sanctuary and Sports Afield biometric gun safes due to serious safety risks. Learn how to address the issue and contact support.

recalls.Description: The CHZHVAN brand combination smoke and carbon monoxide detectors, model JKD-512, are being recalled due to a serious safety issue. These detectors, made of white plastic and approximately four inches in diameter, feature a digital display surrounded by a black plastic circle. The model number and battery specifications are printed on the back, which also accommodates three AAA batteries. Consumers should be aware that these devices may not activate, putting them at risk during a fire emergency.
recalls.Title: Recall of CHZHVAN Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detectors Due to Fire Alert Failure
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Haikouhuidishangmaoyouxiangongs via email at l18276710356@163.com, or visit www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=A4INNIJC6B92S&asin=B0C2ZCTCZ8&ref_=dp_merchant_link&isAmazonFulfilled=1 or www.haikouhuidi.com and navigate to the 'Recalls' section for further details.

Products.Name: CHZHVAN Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector

Images.Caption: Recalled CHZHVAN Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector (Front with Packaging)
Recalled CHZHVAN Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector (Back with Packaging)

Injuries.Name: There are five reports of the detectors failing to alarm in the presence of smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from August 2023 to January 2024, priced between $18 and $59.

Hazards.Name: Risk of non-activation, leading to lack of fire alerts

Remedies.Name: Stop using the detectors and contact Haikouhuidishangmaoyouxiangongsi for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: CHZHVAN smoke and carbon monoxide detectors recalled for failure to alert during fires. Contact for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: Honeywell is recalling all L-Series Low Frequency Sounders, Sounder Strobes, and Compact Sounders from System Sensor due to a potential malfunction that may prevent the fire alarm system from alerting consumers in case of a fire. These devices feature square designs with white and red plastic housings, intended for wall mounting. The sounders and strobes measure approximately 5.6 by 4.7 inches, while the compact sounder measures about 5.3 by 3.5 inches. The model numbers and date codes, specifically 3034 and 3035, can be found on the product labels and packaging.
recalls.Title: Honeywell Recalls System Sensor L-Series Fire Alarm Sounders and Strobes Due to Alert Failure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact System Sensor at 800-736-7672 between 7 a.m. and 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at LFSounder.recall@systemsensor.com or visit https://buildings.honeywell.com/us/en/brands/our-brands/system-sensor/resources/recall or www.SystemSensor.com and click on 'Recall' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled System Sensor L-Series Low Frequency Fire Alarm Sounders Model #s HWL-LF and HWL-LF-BP10 (White) showing product label and carton label
Recalled System Sensor L-Series Low Frequency Fire Alarm Sounders Model #s HWL-LF and HWL-LF-BP10 (Red) showing product label and carton label
Recalled System Sensor L-Series Low Frequency Fire Alarm Sounder Strobe Model P2WL-LF (White) showing product label and carton label
Recalled System Sensor L-Series Low Frequency Fire Alarm Sounder Model HGWL-LF-BP10 (White) showing product label and carton label

Injuries.Name: Honeywell has received two reports of low or no sound output during installation. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Honeywell International Inc., of Charlotte, North Carolina
System Sensor, of St. Charles, Illinois

Retailers.Name: Available through Honeywell authorized System Sensor installers and fire equipment distributors nationwide from March 2023 to April 2023, priced around $107.

Hazards.Name: Potential malfunction leading to failure in fire alarm alerts

Remedies.Name: Free inspection and repair available through the firm

recalls.metaDescription: Honeywell recalls L-Series fire alarm sounders and strobes due to alert failure risk. Contact for free inspection and repair.

recalls.Description: Bulldog Cases has issued a recall for its Magnum Biometric Pistol Vaults, including models with shelves and top load designs. These black steel safes are intended for securely storing firearms and valuables. The dimensions of the pistol vault (model BD4030B) are 11.5 inches by 8 inches by 5.5 inches, while the model with a shelf (BD4040B) measures 11.5 inches by 10 inches by 8 inches. The top load variant (BD4055B) is 11.5 inches by 9.8 inches by 2.5 inches. Each model features a label on a silver sticker located on the back right corner, with the brand name 'Bulldog' prominently displayed on the front.
recalls.Title: Bulldog Cases Recalls Biometric Gun Safes Due to Serious Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Bulldog at 888-799-8001 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email recall@bulldogcases.net or visit https://bulldogcases.com/recall or https://bulldogcases.com and select 'Recall Form' at the top of the page for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bulldog Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault, Model No. BD4030B
Recalled Bulldog Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault with Shelf, Model No. BD4040B
Recalled Bulldog Magnum Biometric Top Load Pistol Vault, Model No. BD4055B

Injuries.Name: The firm received four reports of the safe being opened by an unauthorized user. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Bass Pro Shops, Walmart, firearm retailers nationwide, and online at www.Amazon.com from July 2016 through January 2024, priced between $194 and $216.

Hazards.Name: Risk of unauthorized access due to biometric lock failure, leading to serious injury or death when storing firearms.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the biometric feature, remove batteries, and use the key for firearm storage. Contact Bulldog for a repair kit or a free replacement safe.

recalls.metaDescription: Bulldog Cases recalls biometric gun safes due to serious injury risks. Learn how to get a repair kit or replacement.

recalls.Description: Machir is recalling specific biometric personal safes designed for secure storage of personal belongings. These safes measure approximately 10 inches by 14 inches by 10 inches, and the serial numbers can be found on the lower right corner of the front door. The recall affects safes with serial numbers within several specified ranges, indicating a potential malfunction of the biometric lock that could allow unauthorized access, especially concerning if firearms are stored inside.
recalls.Title: Machir Recalls Biometric Personal Safes Due to Serious Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Machir at 888-505-5195 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at Info@machir.com, or visit https://machir.com/biorecall/ for detailed information on the recall.

Images.Caption: Recalled Machir Biometric Personal Safe
Recalled Machir Biometric Personal Safe with key inserted
Recalled Machir Biometric Personal Safe packaging

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 15 reports of the recalled safes being opened by unauthorized users when the biometric lock failed to secure the safe. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from September 2021 to February 2023, priced between $170 and $400.

Hazards.Name: Risk of unauthorized access due to biometric lock failure, leading to serious injury or death if firearms are stored.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the biometric feature, remove the batteries, and use the key until a free replacement is provided. Machir will offer guidance on disabling the biometric lock and obtaining a replacement safe. A photo of the disabled biometric reader must be emailed to Info@machir.com to receive the new safe. Instructions for safely destroying the biometric reader are available at http://machir.com/biorecall/.

recalls.metaDescription: Machir recalls biometric personal safes due to risks of unauthorized access and serious injury. Contact for free replacement details.

recalls.Description: BBRKIN is recalling its Biometric Gun Safe, model QCJJ01, which is designed for firearm and valuables storage. This gray steel safe measures approximately 13 inches by 14 inches by 15 inches and features one shelf, capable of holding around five firearms. The brand name 'MouTec' is displayed on the front, and the serial number can be found on a label located at the lower right corner of the safe. Affected serial numbers range from SQC202121000 to SQC202247603.
recalls.Title: BBRKIN Biometric Gun Safes Recalled Due to Serious Injury Risks; Sold Exclusively on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BBRKIN via email at support@bbrkin.com or visit www.securitysafe.store/recallreplacement. You can also find more details by clicking on 'Recall Form' at the top of www.securitysafe.store.

Images.Caption: Recalled BBRKIN Biometric Firearm Safe model number QCJJ01

Injuries.Name: The firm received one report of the safe opened by an unauthorized user, a 6-year-old boy. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon from September 2021 to February 2023, priced between $170 and $400.

Importers.Name: Jomani International Inc., of Monterey Park, California

Distributors.Name: Ningbo Moyumaoyi Co. Ltd., dba BBRKIN, of China

Hazards.Name: Unauthorized access to the biometric lock can lead to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the biometric feature immediately, remove the batteries, and use only the key for firearm storage. Contact BBRKIN for a repair kit or a free replacement safe. To receive a replacement, consumers must disable the biometric reader and send a photo of it to support@bbrkin.com. Detailed instructions for disabling the biometric reader are available at www.securitysafe.store/recallreplacement.

recalls.metaDescription: BBRKIN recalls Biometric Gun Safes due to serious injury risks. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Awesafe has issued a recall for specific biometric gun safes that can accommodate two pistols. These safes are black and prominently display the brand name 'Awesafe' on the front. The recall is due to a serious safety concern regarding the biometric lock, which may fail, allowing unauthorized access and posing significant risks of injury or death.
recalls.Title: Awesafe Biometric Gun Safes Recalled Due to Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers should reach out to Awesafe via email at Recall@awesafeus.com or visit https://awesafeus.com/RECALL. Additional information can be found by navigating to the 'RECALL INFORMATION' section on their website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Awesafe Gun Safe - closed
Recalled Awesafe Gun Safe - open

Injuries.Name: The firm has received reports of 71 incidents of the recalled gun safes being opened by unauthorized users when the biometric lock failed to secure the safe. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart stores nationwide and online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com from August 2019 until December 7, 2022, priced around $130.

Importers.Name: Shenghaina Technology Co. Ltd., d/b/a Awesafe, of China

Hazards.Name: Biometric lock failure allowing unauthorized access, leading to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease using the biometric feature, remove batteries, and use the key for secure firearm storage until a free replacement safe is obtained. Contact Awesafe for instructions on disabling the biometric feature and to request a replacement safe. Consumers must disable the biometric reader and send a photo of it to Recall@awesafeus.com to receive their new safe. Detailed instructions for safely disabling the biometric reader are also available at http://awesafeus.com/RECALL. Once the replacement is received, dispose of the recalled safe according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Awesafe recalls biometric gun safes due to serious safety risks. Learn how to disable the lock and obtain a replacement.
